title: [conmon:d]: failed to write to /proc/self/oom_score_adj: Permission denied
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**

```
[…]
[conmon:d]: failed to write to /proc/self/oom_score_adj: Permission denied
[…]
writing file '/proc/11598/gid_map': Operation not permitted\nsetgid(0): Invalid argument: OCI runtime permission denied error"
```
<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

Note: this is **not** a standard Fedora or Silverblue

comments.body:
In a rootless container, you are not allowed to set the oom_score_adj to a Negative number, just like you are not allowed to do it as a normal user
```
$ echo -1 > /proc/self/oom_score_adj 
bash: echo: write error: Permission denied
$ podman run -ti fedora sh -c "echo -1 > /proc/self/oom_score_adj"
sh: line 0: echo: write error: Permission denied
```
so, why is conmon doing it?

We use the same Conmon code when we run as root, and without root. The OOM adjust is the only thing that breaks, so this is generally fine. We don't necessarily need it (it's very helpful in low-memory situations to prevent the critical conmon process from being killed, but under normal circumstances it's not needed. Our approach so far has been to let it log warnings about this, which has so far been easier than actually coding Conmon to not do this when run without root. If someone wants to write a patch to make Conmon not do this when run without root, we'd be happy to take it, but from our perspective it's not harming anything.

Your actual error appears to be
`
time="2020-01-24T09:57:32+01:00" level=error msg="unable to start container \"fedora-toolbox-31\": writing file '/proc/11598/gid_map': Operation not permitted\nsetgid(0): Invalid argument: OCI runtime permission denied error"
`

This looks suspiciously like a newgidmap failure - I would check the permissions of `newuidmap` and `newgidmap` and make sure they look right

```
mheon@Agincourt ~ » getcap /usr/bin/newgidmap 
/usr/bin/newgidmap = cap_setgid+ep
mheon@Agincourt ~ » getcap /usr/bin/newuidmap
/usr/bin/newuidmap = cap_setuid+ep
mheon@Agincourt ~ » ls -l /usr/bin/newuidmap /usr/bin/newgidmap
-rwxr-xr-x. 1 root root 47480 Sep  2 09:52 /usr/bin/newgidmap
-rwxr-xr-x. 1 root root 47464 Sep  2 09:52 /usr/bin/newuidmap
```

```
$ getcap /bin/new*
/bin/newgidmap = cap_setgid+ep
/bin/newuidmap = cap_setuid+ep
$ ll  /bin/new*map
-rwxr-xr-x. 1 root root 47480 24. Jan 10:57 /bin/newgidmap
-rwxr-xr-x. 1 root root 47464 24. Jan 10:57 /bin/newuidmap
```

Ok, solved the issue by:

```
$ rm -fr .local/share/containers
$ rm -fr .config/containers
$ kill $(pidof podman)
```
seems like the default config (from older versions) was broken somehow. Also in my first attempts to clear the config, I forgot to `kill` and it had no effect.

What a mess.

FYI, the same thing happened to us recently and removing the files as suggested in the last comment fixed the issue for us.
podman version: podman-1.6.4-12.module+el8.2.0+6669+dde598ec.aarch64 (on RHEL8)

Hello from the future. Same issue, but different results:
1. getcap outputs nothing and returns 0 (I hope it's good)
2. permissions of the binaries differ:
```
~ ❯ ls -l /bin/new*map
-rws--x--x 1 root root 55640 сен  8 10:07 /bin/newgidmap
-rws--x--x 1 root root 55608 сен  8 10:07 /bin/newuidmap
```
Distro: Gentoo
Podman: 4.5.0
